mnemonics: B.A.D.S. (Broadcasting Alignment, Data Structures) - Remember to check the Broadcasting rules and Alignment of Data Structures before operations
keyPoints: Broadcasting allows operations between arrays of different shapes by expanding smaller arrays to match larger ones.
Index alignment is crucial in pandas operations; DataFrames/Series align on indexes and columns before the operation is performed.
Operations between different data structures (e.g., NumPy array and pandas Series) require careful attention to their inherent properties and potential implicit conversions.
commonMistakes: Assuming index order is the same in different DataFrames, leading to misaligned data after an operation.
Attempting to perform an operation on arrays with incompatible shapes that cannot be broadcast.
Overlooking the difference between in-place operations (e.g., `df['col'] += 1`) and standard operations which return a new object.
